localization.role: NEW module — protocol-agnostic core (state/actions/reducers/SessionHost/shims), from sdd/artifacts/devloop_session_state.py sketch
AHP-style HOST: owns SessionHost registry keyed by run_id; run()/run_revision() open/close sessions; active_runs is the root-channel catalogue seed
dual-publish shim point — resolves run_id per-event from context; host lookup must be registry-based
blocking manual_criterion gates replace passed=True synthesis; resolutions MUST fold into QAReport before return (CEL routes on result.passed)
deployment_approval gate between PR creation and Jira transition (steps 2→3)
adapter target — new view='state': Snapshot on connect, then envelopes from flow:{run_id}:actions
multi-agent layer: each dispatch = terminal-channel occupant; XADD site is the second shim point
nearest precedent for an external command surface; resolve_gate command path does not exist anywhere yet

constraints.claim: One AgentsFlow instance is shared across concurrent runs; per-run SessionHost must be resolved by run_id via a registry on the runner, never captured as a single reference on flow/publisher
CEL edge predicates route on node RESULTS (result.passed), so gate outcomes must be folded into QAReport before QANode.execute returns
FEAT-270 review-fix-rerun loop and degrade-to-pass semantics in qa.py must be preserved; gate integration lands after the review loop
Event publishing must never break a run (publisher swallows all failures, maxlen~10k); host.apply must fold in-memory even when Redis XADD fails

AHP is DRAFT — wire compatibility must stay quarantined behind a future adapter; the stable subset to mirror is: channel-scoped ActionEnvelope{channel, serverSeq, origin, rejectionReason}, Snapshot{resource, state, fromSeq}, reconnect replay-vs-snapshot, root catalogue events, SessionInputNeeded (awaiting_gate) and toolCallReady/Confirmed (gate arbitration)
dev_loop files are under active development (FEAT-270, Moonshot/Z.ai dispatchers); anchors must be re-verified at /sdd-task time

hypotheses.statement: Adopt brainstorm Option B extended one level up per the AHP host model: (L1) session_state.py protocol-agnostic core exactly as sketched, hardened with AHP envelope fields (origin: {client_id, client_seq}, rejection_reason) and an explicit root-channel state (RunRegistryState + RunAdded/RunRemoved/RunSummaryChanged actions) so the runner is a true multi-session HOST; (L2) DevLoopRunner owns Dict[run_id, SessionHost] + a periodic expiry sweep + resolve_gate/cancel command methods; (L3) shims dual-publish from FlowEventPublisher and the dispatchers' XADD path; (L4) QANode + DeploymentHandoffNode open blocking gates; (L5) FlowStreamMultiplexer view='state' serves Snapshot + actions envelopes. Wire protocol (JSON-RPC/auth/ahp-* URIs) stays out of scope for a follow-up adapter spec.
hypotheses.reasoning: Every integration point verified at HEAD; AHP mapping is structural (host/session/terminal/changeset ≅ runner/run/dispatch-stream/PR) and its stable Redux-shaped subset is exactly what the sketch implements; the only architectural correction to the brainstorm is registry-based host resolution forced by the shared-flow concurrency model.

unknowns.question: Blocking manual criteria: opt-in per ManualCriterion (`blocking: bool = False` field on models.py:70) or a flow-level config flag?
options: per-criterion field (default False — preserves current behavior, finer control)
unknowns.user_answer: Per-criterion field: add `blocking: bool = False` to ManualCriterion (models.py:70); default False preserves current behavior

unknowns.question: Scope of the root channel (multi-run catalogue): include parrot-root:// state (RunRegistry + RunAdded/RunRemoved actions + listSessions-like snapshot) in this feature, or keep FEAT-322 session-scoped and defer root to the wire-adapter spec?
options: include root channel now (true AHP host shape; nav-admin run list gets snapshot semantics too)
defer root; session channel only (smaller diff, run list keeps polling active_runs)
unknowns.user_answer: Include root channel now: RunRegistryState + RunAdded/RunRemoved/RunSummaryChanged actions owned by the runner

unknowns.question: Client command transport for resolve_gate / cancel: extend the existing FlowStreamMultiplexer WebSocket with client→server command messages (AHP-like, single socket), or add REST endpoints on the aiohttp app (webhook.py pattern)?
options: WS commands on the multiplexer socket (mirrors AHP dispatchAction; one connection per run)
REST POST /runs/{run_id}/gates/{gate_id}/resolve (simpler auth/audit, no socket state)
both: WS for subscribe/stream, REST for commands
unknowns.user_answer: WS + REST: WS multiplexer socket for subscribe/snapshot/stream; REST POST /runs/{run_id}/gates/{gate_id}/resolve (and cancel) for commands

recommended_next_command.rationale: High-confidence localization, zero anchor drift, design sketch already contract-complete; brainstorm recommends per-spec isolation — spec dev-loop-session-state (core + host + shims + streaming view) first, layering dev-loop-approval-gates on its frozen contracts.
